text:
**Funding sources:**
*   Initial Coin Offering (ICO) raised $10,500,000 USD [cite: 85].
Backed by Techstars [cite: 87].
**Key projects:**
*   Regen Ledger & Regen Registry [cite: 81, 83]
*   CarbonPlus asset [cite: 84]
*   Community Staking DAOs [cite: 83]

**Governance model:**
Operates a Proof-of-Stake consensus mechanism secured by over 80 independent validators.
Governance is shifting heavily toward decentralized Community Staking DAOs guided by the Regen Foundation [cite: 81, 83, 84].
**Community size:**
80+ network validators; over 2 million carbon/biodiversity credits issued; supporting over 15 million hectares of land under ecological regeneration [cite: 83, 84].
---

## Climate TRACE

**Name:** Climate TRACE
**Type:** NGO Coalition
**Orientation:** GARDEN
**Website:** https://climatetrace.org
**Location:** Global
**Scale:** Large
**Maturity:** Mature

**What they do:**
Climate TRACE (Tracking Real-Time Atmospheric Carbon Emissions) is an independent global coalition that monitors and publishes greenhouse gas emissions [cite: 90, 91].
Eschewing delayed, self-reported government data, they combine AI, machine learning, and satellite imagery to provide highly granular, real-time tracking of massive emission sources worldwide (e.g., power plants, steel mills, shipping) [cite: 90, 92].
**Key people:**
*   Al Gore, Former U.S.
Vice President (Partner and core backer) [cite: 90, 92]
*   Kelly Sims Gallagher (Advisor/Commentator) [cite: 90]
*   *(Note: WattTime acts as the secretariat of the coalition)* [cite: 92].
**Partners and collaborators:**
*   WattTime (Secretariat) [cite: 92]
*   TransitionZero [cite: 92]
*   CarbonPlan [cite: 90]
*   Earthrise Alliance [cite: 90]
*   Global Energy Monitor [cite: 90]
*   Hudson Carbon [cite: 90]
*   OceanMind [cite: 90]
*   Rocky Mountain Institute [cite: 90]
*   Blue Sky Analytics [cite: 90]
*   Hypervine [cite: 90]
*   The Climate Group [cite: 92]
*   Johns Hopkins Applied Physics Laboratory [cite: 93]

**Events they run or attend:**
*   UNFCCC COP26, COP27 [cite: 90, 92]

**Publications, reports, or open-source tools:**
*   **Open-source tools:** Free, interactive global map of greenhouse gas emission sources; API/datasets covering 10 sectors and over 50 subsectors [cite: 90, 91, 92].
**Funding sources:**
*   Google.org (Initial 2019 AI Impact Challenge grant of $1.7 million) [cite: 92, 94]
*   Bloomberg Philanthropies [cite: 94]

**Key projects:**
*   Asset-level global emissions inventory mapping over 352 million assets [cite: 92]
*   States and Regions Remote Sensing (STARRS) project [cite: 92]

**Community size:**
A collaborative coalition of over 100 nonprofits, universities, tech companies, and researchers [cite: 92].
---

## Rainforest Connection (RFCx)

**Name:** Rainforest Connection (RFCx)
**Type:** Company / Non-profit
**Orientation:** GARDEN
**Website:** https://rfcx.org
**Location:** Global (HQ Katy, Texas, USA)
**Scale:** Medium
**Maturity:** Mature

**What they do:**
Rainforest Connection creates and deploys bio-acoustic monitoring systems in global rainforests.
By repurposing smartphones equipped with solar panels into "Guardian" listening stations, they utilize AI to analyze forest soundscapes in real-time.
If sounds of illegal logging (chainsaws, trucks) or poaching (gunshots, dogs) are detected, immediate alerts are sent to rangers on the ground, enabling rapid physical intervention [cite: 95, 96, 97].
**Key people:**
*   Topher White, Founder [cite: 96]
*   Bourhan Yassin, CEO [cite: 95, 98]
*   Antony Harfield, CTO [cite: 95]
*   Sarika Khanwilkar, Head of Partnerships [cite: 95]
*   Ashlee Taylor, Marketing Coordinator [cite: 95]

**Partners and collaborators:**
*   Arm [cite: 97]

**Publications, reports, or open-source tools:**
*   **Open-source tools/platforms:** *RFCx Guardian System*, *RFCx Arbimon* (Biodiversity Monitoring system), *RFCx App* (Live audio streams and recordings accessible to the public) [cite: 96, 97].
**Key projects:**
*   Anti-logging and anti-poaching threat detection grids [cite: 96]
*   Comprehensive acoustic biodiversity monitoring programs enabling adaptive management [cite: 97]

**Community size:**
Monitoring systems are currently deployed across more than 22 countries, including Indonesia, Ecuador, Peru, and Brazil.
They have collected over 47 million minutes of audio data [cite: 96, 97].
---

## Naturskyddsföreningen

**Name:** Naturskyddsföreningen (Swedish Society for Nature Conservation)
**Type:** NGO
**Orientation:** GARDEN
**Website:** https://www.naturskyddsforeningen.se
**Location:** Stockholm, Sweden
**Scale:** Very Large
**Maturity:** Highly Mature
**Flags:** Nordic/Swedish Connection

**What they do:**
Naturskyddsföreningen is Sweden's oldest and largest environmental non-profit organization, established in 1909.
They spread knowledge, map environmental threats, influence policy, and devise practical solutions around climate, oceans, forests, toxins, and agriculture [cite: 99, 100, 101].
They heavily leverage consumer power to enforce corporate responsibility through strict ecolabeling standards.
**Key people:**
*   Karin Lexén, General Secretary [cite: 100]
*   Johanna Sandahl, Chairman [cite: 100]
*   Mikael Karlsson, Former Chairman [cite: 100]
*   Erik Halkjær, Editor-in-Chief of *Sveriges Natur* [cite: 100]
*   Rutger Sernander, Founder [cite: 100]
*   Sten Selander, Former Chairman [cite: 100]

**Partners and collaborators:**
*   Swedish Environmental Protection Agency [cite: 100]
*   Swedish Ornithological Society [cite: 100]

**Events they run or attend:**
*   Annual local chapters and national assemblies [cite: 102]
*   Miljövänliga veckan ("Eco-friendly Week" initiated in 1990) [cite: 100]

**Publications, reports, or open-source tools:**
*   *Sveriges Natur* (Member magazine published since 1910) [cite: 100, 102]

**Funding sources:**
*   Primarily funded via membership dues and donations; recorded collections of ~166 million SEK in 2024 and ~151 million SEK in 2022 [cite: 99].
Co-manages the Alvin Fund [cite: 100].
